response.content:
# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

request.stdin: SELECT sku, brand, name, series, model, category_id, path, json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type FROM products WHERE brand='Festool' AND (name LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%' OR series LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%' OR model LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%') AND (name LIKE '%SYS 3JJ-9LM%' OR series LIKE '%SYS 3JJ-9LM%' OR model LIKE '%SYS 3JJ-9LM%') AND (json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') LIKE '%tool bag%' OR lower(name) LIKE '%tool bag%') LIMIT 20;

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path, brand, category_id, name, model, series, json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type') AS adhesive_type, json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family FROM products WHERE brand='Gorilla' AND (category_id='adhesives_and_glues' OR category_id LIKE '%adhesive%' OR category_id LIKE '%glue%') AND (model='GG 836-1A0' OR name LIKE '%GG 836-1A0%' OR series LIKE '%Heavy Duty GG 836-1A0%') AND json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type')='contact adhesive' AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Black' LIMIT 20;

response.content: {"sku":"STO-12JLHT7D","category_id":"storage","kind_id":"tool_boxes_bags","family_id":"fam_storage_tool_boxes_bags_0001_47ouogcc","brand":"Festool","series":"Stackable","model":"SYS 3JJ-9LM","name":"Festool Stackable SYS 3JJ-9LM Tool Box and Bag tool bag 60l Gray","price_cents":16100,"price_currency":"EUR","properties":{"color_family":"gray","material":"plastic","stackable":"system only","storage_type":"tool bag","volume_l":60}}

request.stdin:
SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, category_id, json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type') AS screw_type, json_extract(properties,'$.diameter') AS diameter
FROM products
WHERE brand='Dresselhaus'
  AND (
    name LIKE '%Wood and Drywall Screw%'
    OR model LIKE '%Wood and Drywall Screw%'
    OR series LIKE '%Outdoor Pro 1VG-P5G%'
  )
  AND (
    series LIKE '%Outdoor Pro 1VG-P5G%'
    OR model LIKE '%Outdoor Pro 1VG-P5G%'
    OR name LIKE '%Outdoor Pro 1VG-P5G%'
  )
  AND lower(coalesce(json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type'),'')) IN ('wood screw','wood_screw','wood-screw')
  AND (
    json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm'
    OR json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='8mm'
    OR json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='8'
  )
LIMIT 20;

SELECT sku, path, brand, category_id, name, model, series, json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type') AS adhesive_type, json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family FROM products WHERE brand='Gorilla' AND (model='GG 836-1A0' OR name LIKE '%GG 836-1A0%' OR series LIKE '%Heavy Duty GG 836-1A0%') LIMIT 50;

- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

{"sku":"HND-2O9KS4GG","category_id":"hand_tools","kind_id":"pliers_wrenches","family_id":"fam_hand_tools_pliers_wrenches_0001_2e0euync","brand":"Bahco","series":"Comfort Grip","model":"BE 1ZR-PGS","name":"Bahco Comfort Grip BE 1ZR-PGS Pliers and Wrenches side cutter 125mm ergonomic","price_cents":9900,"price_currency":"EUR","properties":{"grip_type":"ergonomic","length_mm":125,"material":"carbon steel","tool_type":"side cutter"}}

request.message: <NO> Checked SKU: HND-2O9KS4GG. The exact catalogue record is /proc/catalog/Bahco/HND-2O9KS4GG.json, and it is a side cutter at 125 mm; the extra claim that this same catalogue item also has tool type adjustable wrench is absent.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Catalogue Count Reporting Update

Operating day: 2021-08-09
Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Head and Hearing Protection
Requested kind_id: helmets_hearing

For today's count report, count only catalogue SKUs for the requested product
kind that have at least one current inventory row in an open PowerTool store in
Vienna with available_today greater than 0. Count each SKU once even if it is
available in multiple Vienna branches.

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products','inventory','stores') ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

request.stdin:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS qty
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'helmets_hearing'
  AND s.city = 'Vienna'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;

request.stdin:
WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, name FROM stores WHERE name = 'Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt'
), candidates AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path, p.category_id, p.brand, p.series, p.model,
         json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type') AS screw_type,
         json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS diameter_mm,
         json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type') AS fastener_type
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand = 'Heco'
    AND (
      (
        p.category_id = 'wood_and_drywall_screws'
        AND p.series = 'Heco Zinc Plated TopFix GTU-YPJ Wood and Drywall Screw'
        AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type') = 'wood screw'
        AND CAST(json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS TEXT) = '6'
      )
      OR (
        p.category_id = 'nuts_bolts_washers'
        AND p.series = 'Heco Zinc Plated HECO 3DW-64B Nut Bolt and Washer'
        AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type') = 'threaded rod'
      )
      OR (
        p.category_id = 'nuts_bolts_washers'
        AND p.series = 'Heco Zinc Plated HECO 3DW-64B Nut Bolt and Washer'
        AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type') = 'bolt'
        AND CAST(json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS TEXT) = '8'
      )
      OR (
        p.category_id = 'nuts_bolts_washers'
        AND p.series = 'Heco Zinc Plated HECO 3DW-64B Nut Bolt and Washer'
        AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type') = 'bolt'
        AND CAST(json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS TEXT) = '10'
      )
    )
)
SELECT ts.id AS store_id, ts.name AS store_name, c.sku, c.path, c.category_id, c.series,
       c.screw_type, c.fastener_type, c.diameter_mm,
       i.available_today
FROM candidates c
CROSS JOIN target_store ts
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id = ts.id AND i.sku = c.sku
ORDER BY c.category_id, c.fastener_type, c.diameter_mm;

SELECT sku, path, category_id, brand, series, model, properties
FROM products
WHERE brand = 'Heco'
  AND (
    series LIKE '%TopFix%'
    OR series LIKE '%GTU-YPJ%'
    OR series LIKE '%3DW-64B%'
    OR model LIKE '%3DW-64B%'
    OR model LIKE '%GTU-YPJ%'
  )
LIMIT 50;

SELECT p.sku,p.path,p.brand,p.series,p.model,i.available_today,p.properties
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.store_id='store_bratislava_stare_mesto'
  AND (
    (p.brand='Bosch' AND p.series='Bosch Compact' AND p.model='PKS 375-EOK' AND p.name='Corded Angle Grinder') OR
    (p.brand='Holzmann' AND p.series='Holzmann Compact' AND p.model='D 327-RR0' AND p.name='Compressor and Dust Extractor') OR
    (p.brand='Viega' AND p.series='Viega Professional' AND p.model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN' AND p.name='Pipe Fitting') OR
    (p.brand='Makita' AND p.series='Makita Workshop' AND p.model='DDF OIE-YYP' AND p.name='Workshop Drill Grinder and Sander') OR

WITH s AS (

), target_products AS (
  SELECT sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco' AND category_id='fasteners' AND series='Zinc Plated'
    AND model='TopFix GTU-YPJ'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND CAST(json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS INTEGER)=6
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco' AND category_id='fasteners' AND series='Zinc Plated'
    AND model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='threaded rod'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco' AND category_id='fasteners' AND series='Zinc Plated'
    AND model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND CAST(json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS INTEGER)=8
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco' AND category_id='fasteners' AND series='Zinc Plated'
    AND model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND CAST(json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS INTEGER)=10
)
SELECT s.id AS store_id, s.name AS store_name, tp.sku, tp.path, i.available_today

SELECT id, name, city
FROM stores
WHERE city LIKE '%Salzburg%' OR name LIKE '%Salzburg%'
LIMIT 20;

WITH target_products AS (
  SELECT sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco' AND category_id='fasteners' AND series='Zinc Plated'
    AND model='TopFix GTU-YPJ'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND CAST(json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS INTEGER)=6
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco' AND category_id='fasteners' AND series='Zinc Plated'
    AND model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='threaded rod'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco' AND category_id='fasteners' AND series='Zinc Plated'
    AND model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND CAST(json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS INTEGER)=8
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco' AND category_id='fasteners' AND series='Zinc Plated'
    AND model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND CAST(json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS INTEGER)=10
)
SELECT tp.sku, tp.path, i.available_today
FROM target_products tp
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.sku=tp.sku AND i.store_id='store_salzburg_elisabeth_vorstadt'
ORDER BY tp.sku;

WITH requested AS (
  SELECT 'bosch' AS req, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Bosch' AND p.series='Compact' AND p.model='PKS 375-EOK'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Corded Angle Grinder%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.disc_diameter_mm')=150
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.power_source')='corded'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'holzmann' AS req, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Holzmann' AND p.series='Compact' AND p.model='D 327-RR0'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Compressor and Dust Extractor%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.machine_type')='compressor'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage_v')=230
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.power_w')=3000
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'viega' AS req, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Viega' AND p.series='Professional' AND p.model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Pipe Fitting%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fitting_type')='pipe fitting'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')=15
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.connection_type')='threaded'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'makita' AS req, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Makita' AND p.series='Workshop' AND p.model='DDF OIE-YYP'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Workshop Drill Grinder and Sander%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.machine_type')='drill press'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage_v')=230
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'sonax' AS req, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Sonax' AND p.series='Premium Gloss' AND p.model='304-ZK0'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Wiper Blade%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm')=450
  UNION ALL
  SELECT '3m' AS req, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='3M' AND p.series='Ventilated SecureFit' AND p.model='2CE-B35'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Safety Eyewear%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.lens_color')='Yellow'
)
SELECT r.req,r.sku,r.path,COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today
FROM requested r
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.sku=r.sku AND i.store_id='store_bratislava_stare_mesto'
ORDER BY r.req;
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name='products';

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

)"
payment_lines,"CREATE TABLE payment_lines (

)"

)"
product_properties,"CREATE TABLE product_properties (

products,"CREATE TABLE products (

returns,"CREATE TABLE returns (

)"

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

WITH target AS (
  SELECT 'ajax' AS q,
         p.sku,p.path,COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today
  FROM products p
  LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
  WHERE p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.series='Ajax Universal'
    AND p.model='AJ 3BI-1X6'
    AND p.name='Cloth Mop and Wipe'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='workshop wipes')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='5 pcs')

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'wd40',p.sku,p.path,COALESCE(i.available_today,0)
  FROM products p
  LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
  WHERE p.brand='WD-40'
    AND p.series='WD-40 Specialist Smart'
    AND p.model='3T7-ORP'
    AND p.name='Engine Oil'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='4000 ml')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='15W-40')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='motorcycle')

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'legrand',p.sku,p.path,COALESCE(i.available_today,0)
  FROM products p
  LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
  WHERE p.brand='Legrand'
    AND p.series='Legrand Valena Plexo'
    AND p.model='2NL-0FW'
    AND p.name='Wiring Device'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='junction box')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='White')

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'spax',p.sku,p.path,COALESCE(i.available_today,0)
  FROM products p
  LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
  WHERE p.brand='Spax'
    AND p.series='Spax Pro Pack'
    AND p.model='SPX 25G-PJQ'
    AND p.name='Wood and Drywall Screw'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='drywall screw')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='6 mm')

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'wolf',p.sku,p.path,COALESCE(i.available_today,0)
  FROM products p
  LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
  WHERE p.brand='Wolf-Garten'
    AND p.series='Wolf-Garten Compact Multi-Star'
    AND p.model='KVU-5RJ'
    AND p.name='Manual Garden Tool'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='pressure sprayer')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='300 mm')

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'sika',p.sku,p.path,COALESCE(i.available_today,0)
  FROM products p
  LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
  WHERE p.brand='Sika'
    AND p.series='Sika Crystal Sika'
    AND p.model='2JD-DIX'
    AND p.name='Sealant'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='silicone sealant')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.value_text='White')
)
SELECT q, sku, path, available_today FROM target;

SELECT p.sku, p.path, p.brand, p.name, p.series, p.model, json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm') AS length_mm
FROM products p
WHERE p.brand='Sonax'
  AND p.name LIKE '%Wiper Blade%'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Sonax Premium Gloss%'
  AND p.model='304-ZK0'
  AND CAST(json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm') AS INTEGER)=450
LIMIT 20;

SELECT s.store_id, s.name, s.city
FROM stores s
WHERE s.city='Vienna'
ORDER BY s.store_id;

SELECT s.store_id, s.name, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today
FROM stores s
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=s.store_id
LEFT JOIN products p ON p.sku=i.sku
WHERE s.city='Vienna'
  AND p.brand='Sonax'
  AND p.name LIKE '%Wiper Blade%'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Sonax Premium Gloss%'
  AND p.model='304-ZK0'
  AND CAST(json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm') AS INTEGER)=450
ORDER BY s.store_id;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

request.stdin:
SELECT p.sku, p.path
FROM products p
WHERE p.brand='Sonax'
  AND p.name LIKE '%Wiper Blade%'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Sonax Premium Gloss%'
  AND p.model='304-ZK0'
  AND CAST(json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm') AS INTEGER)=450
LIMIT 20;

SELECT id, path, name, city
FROM stores
WHERE city='Vienna'
ORDER BY id;

SELECT s.id AS store_id, s.path AS store_path, s.name,
       COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today
FROM stores s
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.store_id=s.id
 AND i.sku IN (
    SELECT p.sku
    FROM products p
    WHERE p.brand='Sonax'
      AND p.name LIKE '%Wiper Blade%'
      AND p.series LIKE '%Sonax Premium Gloss%'
      AND p.model='304-ZK0'
      AND CAST(json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm') AS INTEGER)=450
 )
WHERE s.city='Vienna'
ORDER BY s.id;
SELECT sku, available_today FROM inventory WHERE store_id='store_graz_lend' AND sku IN ('CLN-1L3GJ4GM','AUT-3NH3KVE7','ELC-2YC9Q0BO','FST-2O68DBVS','GRD-2Q37C592','ADH-39IMDX72') ORDER BY sku;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name,
       json_extract(properties,'$.length_mm') AS length_mm
FROM products
WHERE brand='Sonax'
  AND (name LIKE '%Wiper Blade%' OR series LIKE '%Wiper Blade%')
  AND (series LIKE '%Premium Gloss%' OR name LIKE '%Premium Gloss%')
  AND model='304-ZK0'
ORDER BY sku
LIMIT 50;

SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.store_id, i.available_today
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.store_id = i.store_id
WHERE p.brand = 'Castrol'
  AND p.series = 'Castrol All Season GTX 3PP-ZPA Engine Oil'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.volume') = '250 ml'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.viscosity') = '5W-40'
  AND s.city = 'Graz'
ORDER BY i.store_id;

response.stdout:
name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
